body.title: Daemon supervisor: exponential backoff on restart; verify one_shot tagging on critical threads
body.result: Shipped in 86033984. New restart_guard.py ledger rate-limits restart.flag (default 5/600s, env-tunable); overflow writes restart_halt.flag and exits 0 instead of 42. Fixed one_shot=True on ne-worker + consolidation-worker registrations — they were in _DEFAULT_CRITICAL but the poll loop skipped them as one_shot, so the safety mechanism was disabled for exactly the threads it was meant to guard. 9 tests.
body.description:
Pass-2 Area 4 P1-8.4a (SHIP). Two coupled issues in daemon_supervisor + main.py:

(1) No backoff on restart.flag — a critical thread that dies within poll_interval of every boot triggers an infinite restart loop with no observable signal beyond churn.

(2) main.py:6932 and 7005 register ne-worker + consolidation-worker with one_shot=True, but they're also in _DEFAULT_CRITICAL. The _poll_loop explicitly skips alerts for one_shot threads (daemon_supervisor.py:145), so critical-thread restart detection was silently disabled for the only two threads it was meant to guard.

**Affected files:** wild_igor/igor/restart_guard.py (new — testable backoff+halt logic), wild_igor/igor/main.py (ne-worker + consolidation-worker register calls drop one_shot=True; restart.flag branch calls record_and_check/write_halt instead of inline logic).
**Design rules:** docs-in-code, test-plan-or-why-not, oop-first (functional shape fits a stateless file-rotating ledger).
**Scope boundary:** IN — restart rate-limit (default 5 per 600s, env-tunable IGOR_MAX_RESTARTS + IGOR_RESTART_HALT_WINDOW_SECS); one_shot audit on the two critical workers. OUT — removing the restart path; changing poll_interval or _DEFAULT_CRITICAL; adding new critical threads.
**Test plan:** 9 tests for restart_guard — first restart never halts; halt fires at N+1; window-aged entries drop off; history persisted; corrupt/non-list history resets; halt flag contents; halt_present default; clear_history. main.py import sanity.
